# passbolt_user (Data Source)

Looks up an existing non-deleted Passbolt user by exact username (email address). By default, only active users are returned.

## Example Usage

```terraform
provider "passbolt" {
  base_url    = "https://passbolt.example.com"
  private_key = file("~/.secrets/passbolt_private_key.pem")
  passphrase  = "mysecurepassphrase"
}

# 🔍 Lookup an existing user by exact email (must be active and not deleted in Passbolt)
data "passbolt_user" "lead" {
  username = "lead.dev@example.com"
}

# 👥 Create a group with this user as manager
resource "passbolt_group" "dev_team" {
  name     = "Development Team"
  managers = [data.passbolt_user.lead.id]
}

# 📁 Create folder and share with the group
resource "passbolt_folder" "shared" {
  name = "Dev Shared Secrets"
}

resource "passbolt_folder_permission" "dev_access" {
  folder_id  = passbolt_folder.shared.id
  group_name = passbolt_group.dev_team.name
  permission = "update"
}
```
-> This data source returns only exact, active, non-deleted users by default. Set `include_inactive = true` only when you intentionally need an inactive regular member UUID for flows that support inactive users.

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `username` (String) Exact username (email address) to look up. The user must already be active unless include_inactive is enabled.

### Optional

- `include_inactive` (Boolean) When true, allows returning an inactive, non-deleted Passbolt user. Inactive users are useful only in flows that explicitly support them, such as regular passbolt_group members with ignore_inactive_members enabled.

### Read-Only

- `active` (Boolean) Whether the Passbolt user has completed activation.
- `first_name` (String) First name of the user.
- `id` (String) User ID (UUID).
- `last_name` (String) Last name of the user.
- `role` (String) Role of the user: admin or user.
